content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 8, 1994

Honorable J. Mark Nielsen
Supervisor, District III
El Dorado County Board of Supervisors
330 Fair Lane
Placerville, CA 95667

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-181

Dear Supervisor Nielsen:

This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of telephone advice provided by me to you on February 24, 1994, regarding your responsibilities as a member of the El Dorado County Board of Supervisors under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

To very briefly summarize the facts that you presented,² you are a member of the El Dorado County Board of Supervisors and are also an attorney with a practice in the jurisdiction. The county has been sued by an individual for wrongful termination, as well as a variety of other charges. Prior to your election to the board of supervisors, the individual had sought to hire you to represent him against the county in the same matter. You held discussions with the individual, but you did not take the case nor did you receive any income from him.

This letter confirms the advice provided to you on February 24, 1994, that absent a financial interest in the decisions in question (as set forth in Section 87103) the Act would not prohibit your participation in the decisions. However, as we discussed, the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of the Act. The Commission does not advise on other laws that might apply to your facts.

________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954.

² This letter is based on the facts presented to us. The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

OUR File No. A-94-181
Page 2


Your question was whether the Act prohibits your
participation in discussions and decisions of the Board of
Supervisors concerning the lawsuit.

As we discussed, pursuant to Section 87103, you have a
financial interest in any decision that will have a reasonably
foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on you, your immediate family, or
on:

1. Any business entity in which you have an investment worth
$1,000 or more, or in which you are a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or hold any position of management. (Section
87103(a) and (d).)

2. Any real property in which you have an interest worth
$1,000 or more. (Section 87103(d).)

3. Sources of $250 or more in income and donors of gifts
aggregating $250 or more received by or promised to you within the
12 months preceding a decision. (Section 87103(c) and (e).)

According to your facts, you have not received, nor have you
been promised, any income from the plaintiff in the litigation.
Absent some economic interest in the decision, the Act does not
prohibit your participation in future decisions pertaining to the
litigation.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.³

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division


³ Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
